item.title: McLaren conquered F1. Can it end a 50-year wait for Indy 500 glory?
item.description: McLaren hasn't won Indy since 1976. But with four cars, a new team boss, and Pato O'Ward hungry, Sunday could finally change that

item.title: Andrea Stella reaffirms McLaren future amid Lambiase succession speculation
item.description: Rumors about Stella's future with the reigning champions began brewing last month.

item.title: Lando Norris' mindful collab. Plus: Kimi Antonelli leads early in Canada
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Williams driver Alex Albon crashes after hitting groundhog in Canadian GP practice
item.description: Williams confirmed that the incident occurred as Albon "came around turn seven and unfortunately couldn’t avoid a groundhog" on the track.

item.title: Mick Schumacher swapped F1 for the Indy 500. But first, he milked a cow
item.description: For a driver raised in the F1 paddock, IndyCar's ovals, traditions and 350,000-strong crowd represent the biggest adjustment of his career

item.title: Lewis Hamilton vows he'll race in F1 'for quite some time, so get used to it'
item.description: Lewis Hamilton has shrugged off suggestions he's contemplating retirement from F1 ahead of the 2026 Canadian GP

item.title: Esteban Ocon says rumors of Haas F1 exit 'complete bulls---'
item.description: The Frenchman also called rumors of a fallout with team boss Ayao Komatsu "complete nonsense."

item.title: Is F1 broken? Fans weigh in on the new cars, the racing and whether to keep watching
item.description: Readers sounded off on the new regulations. The verdict: F1 is more complicated, more divisive and almost nobody is looking away

item.title: Author John Green’s Indy 500 tradition includes a bike ride, a beer and Turn 2 seats
item.description: Author John Green makes an annual pilgrimage to the Indianapolis 500, to nurture his roots in his home town

item.title: McLaren's CEO brushes off Red Bull pursuit of Oscar Piastri: 'Not a team on the grid that wouldn't'
item.description: Piastri is on a long-term contract at McLaren but reports have linked Red Bull with a move for the Australian if Max Verstappen departs.

item.title: Inside Cadillac's first F1 home race – and the slow, relentless work of a new team
item.description: Inside the team's Miami GP garage, upgrades and debriefs showed signs Cadillac is slowly finding its F1 footing

item.title: The race George Russell must win. Plus: Why we're all Max Verstappen-obsessed
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Other sports have strict rules against multi-team ownership. Why doesn’t F1?
item.description: Many major sports have clear rules that prevent organizations owning multiple teams, so why is this not the case in Formula 1?

item.title: Disillusioned with F1, Max Verstappen is about to start his longest motor race yet
item.description: Max Verstappen has expanded his interest in competing outside F1 in recent years. Now he's taking on his first 24-hour sports car race

item.title: Happy 10 years, Max Verstappen! Plus: Why the Canadian GP is important
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: Valtteri Bottas reveals he had Cadillac Escalade and F1 paddock pass stolen during Miami GP weekend
item.description: The 36-year-old confirmed that the road car had later been found abandoned.

item.title: Montreal strippers call for strike during Canadian Grand Prix weekend
item.description: The Sex Work Autonomous Committee said that the grand prix weekend is "the most lucrative period of the year for our boss".

item.title: The oral history of Max Verstappen’s first F1 win: 'Yo ho! How bizarre!'
item.description: The youngest winner in F1 history, and the people around him, look back on the race that started a dynasty

item.title: F1 engines will change in 2027. But details are not final and some teams could lose out
item.description: F1 stakeholders have agreed to change engines again in 2027. But many arguments still need to be settled before the final rules are set

item.title: Who could replace Max Verstappen at Red Bull? Plus: Why both parties might look elsewhere
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: F1 mailbag: Where in the world is Adrian Newey? And is George Russell in trouble?
item.description: Kimi Antonelli's third consecutive victory has turned up the heat on his teammate

item.title: New regulations show progress, not perfection. Plus: Our expanded motorsport coverage
item.description: Welcome to Prime Tire, The Athletic's F1 newsletter.

item.title: F1 is discussing rescheduling cancelled Middle East races. But any new date has issues
item.description: Top F1 management figures have suggested one of the cancelled Bahrain or Saudi Arabian races could return in 2026. But plans remain fluid

item.title: Trading card market hit record highs (again) in April: Cooper Flagg and Pokémon drive sales
item.description: The trading card market remains hotter than ever as athletes in a variety of sports excite collectors.

item.title: Ford confirms talks with Max Verstappen about future Le Mans appearance
item.description: Mark Rushbrook, the global director of Ford Racing, also defended the FIA's plans to change engine designs from 2027 to "help racing".

item.title: Welcome to F1's new era of 'speed chess,' where every pass comes with a price
item.description: DRS is out. Overtake Mode is in. Here's why drivers say passing just got a lot harder

item.title: Why F1's boiling drama over engine compression ratios matters for the 2026 season
item.description: Has Mercedes found a loophole with its engines? And what exactly is the loophole, anyway?

item.title: F1's new cars behave unlike any others. It's all down to new aerodynamics
item.description: F1 has new cars in 2026, which look different to previous eras in grand prix racing. Here's how they work and what the drivers think

item.title: F1 hits reset in 2026. Your guide to the new cars, new rules and new era
item.description: Formula 1 evolves dramatically in 2026. Here's your year-long guide to it all

item.title: F1's new engines are the sport's biggest gamble in 12 years. Here's what changed
item.description: F1 is introducing new hybrid engines ahead of the 2026 season. Here's how they work and why they matter

item.title: How Formula 1's cost cap works: Rules, penalties and past breaches explained
item.description: The sport's $135 million spending limit aims to create parity — but compliance isn't always simple

item.title: F1 penalty points: How they work and why they're needed even though drivers have doubts
item.description: A deep dive into the details - and arguments - of Formula One penalty points for driver infractions

item.title: What are F1 driver development programs? Inside the academies that nurture future champions
item.description: All of F1's new 2025 drivers have one thing in common - they were part of a team’s driver development program or academy.

item.title: What are F1’s racing rules? A look at race stewards and drivers' battle for clarity
item.description: Multiple times during the 2024 season, drivers have faced questions — and raised their own — about what is and is not allowed on track.

item.title: How F1's Red Bull mastered the art of the 2-second pit stop
item.description: A deep dive into the positions, the practice, and the fine-tuned production that is the Formula One pit stop.

item.title: Welcome to Between the Racing Lines, your new guide to all things F1
item.description: F1 is complicated and confusing at times, and is constantly evolving. Here's your guide to keeping up.

item.title: Sim time, data crunching and Candy Crush: The life of an F1 reserve driver
item.description: Reserve drivers are more than simple substitutes — they play important roles within their teams, even if they rarely get to hit the track.

item.title: How F1 drivers train physically and mentally to go from 'gym fit' to 'race fit'
item.description: To have a chance at the title, drivers need not just a top-caliber car but to extract the maximum performance from it — and themselves.
